item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels too close the what I dislike about AWCY? even if it's not the intended point. It seems overt anomalous art is always centered around "modern art" one way or another. I would like to see more SCP about anartists that use their abilities for something they enjoy. There's SCPs that have what would otherwise be anomalous art (movies, books, paintings, etc…) but that don't really explore the artistic part of it. The intentions of the artists, what it means to them, the process of creation, etc… would be interesting aspects of art to explore through SCPs that aren't depressing or dangerous. Maybe it's me forgetting articles or not reading the right ones, but a lot of the stuff that seems to get close to exploring artists tends to be kinda depressing or generally negative. A more positive outlook of art would be welcome.

SCP-4390 is about a guy making a real life version of a board game because he likes that stuff, and there's no dead participants. I find that type exploration of artists and art much more interesting than grabbing the cliched elitists "modern artists" and making them malicious.

Kalinin's Metacritique 2 does a way better job of explaining my point, and there's a quote that I really like.

+ show block
– hide block

Just imagine the things that artists could do if the strictures of what is possible are completely lifted. What if Michelangelo were not bound by the practical limitations of the dimensions and accessibility of the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel? What kind of journey could a Kiowa shaman provide if he had something more powerful than mere peyote to dispense? What wonders could the pharaohs have commanded if the laws of physics were optional? What kind of experience awaits the audience of a Noh school that has adapted its structure beyond the ordinary costuming and staging limitations of consensus reality?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would think killing multiple people is the worst of those two cases. You know, murder and all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like an experiment log wouldn't go into detail about how the game of Smash Bros was lost… instead of explaining that the subject fell off the stage and forgot his character's moves, and that the AI was level 3, the log should just say that he performed very poorly against an easy challenge

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Depends on your viewpoint/headcanon, I guess. They kill dozens of people, but they wouldn't go ahead and just make someone be unable to enjoy art again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure AWCY? has often been characterized as doing *much* worse for *much* less reason, no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, but even if the rest of the article was mediocre, I'd still upvote it for the fact that the guy knew he would destroy his own talent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically yeah, it was meant as elitism in art. I don't think it really mocks AWCY?, as Alex is kind of a hypocritical asshole who turns around and demeans others as "not real art."

Also, he goes one step further and just removes one of the most important things in their lives. As bad as AWCY? is, they wouldn't do something like that just because someone pissed them off. I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me, this does work as a sort of commentary on elitism in art (at least that's how I understood it). Alex caring more about preventing art he disapproves than making art himself feels particularly fitting. Articles about people mocking/criticizing AWCY? are quite abundant at this point, but I do like what this particular article does.

Also, I would have enjoyed seeing more of how people affected by the anomaly actually deal with the loss of their abilities (particularly someone like a member of AWCY, who would likely define themselves based on their artistry). However, given the direction the article takes, I can see that it may not have fit with the intended story very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels spiteful in a way that many bad AWCY articles are, and the interview at the end is essentially just a reskinned Note at the End which conveniently explains the anomaly’s purpose instead of giving Alex Nguyen a character beyond spiteful artist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1+

I like this. I really, really do. It's horror, but not the squicky kind. Losing your ability to do some that brings you joy, something you make a living with or something you're simply good at is a special kind of fear. What makes it even worse is this can easily happen via non-anomalous means. Brain injuries can happen to anyone. The symptoms from exposure appear to match.

Also, I enjoyed seeing a GOI (especially one like AWCY) feeling the logical consequences of their actions. If you're a dick, you're eventually going to piss someone off who can harm you in return. And they may just not care they'll get caught in crossfire.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
For neither man nor angel can discern hypocrisy, the only evil that walks invisible, except to God alone.

A SCP dealing with art, hypocrites, and psychopaths. Thank you to everyone for reading this, and be sure to check out some of my other works!

Photo is from here.
